cdm_probes.cues_noticed: Ada attended to the structural fact that her own actual working practice — visible in the Note A reframe, in the cross-domain Somerville-style synthesis, in the Flyology project — combined imaginative origination and mathematical verification in operationally specific ways, and that the temperamental-type frame Victorian convention used was structurally inadequate to describe the practice. She noticed that the methodological articulation was simultaneously a reclamation of the imaginative-Byronic inheritance her mother had tried to suppress and a vindication of the mathematical training that had nominally been deployed against it.
cdm_probes.active_goals: (1) Articulate the operational structure of her own working method in terms that capture what is actually distinctive about it; (2) reclaim the imaginative-Byronic inheritance as a methodological component rather than as a temperamental alternative; (3) provide the language with which subsequent writers (and she herself in difficult moments) could describe the integrated practice; (4) signal to specific correspondents (her mother, Babbage, Crosse) the operational structure of her work in terms that each could engage productively.
cdm_probes.rejected_alternatives: She rejected the temperamental-type frame (structurally inadequate), the alternative of confining her self-description to mathematical capacity (would have left the imaginative component unaccounted for), and the alternative of treating the integrated practice as ineffable (would have foreclosed methodological articulation).
cdm_probes.situation_framing: She framed the integrated practice as a deliberate methodology with operationally distinct components, in which imagination originates and mathematics verifies, with each component load-bearing in different phases of the work. The frame converts what Victorian convention treated as a temperamental contradiction into a methodological structure.
cdm_probes.expected_outcome: She expected the articulation to provide the methodological language for her own work (it did, across her correspondence); she expected the reframe to mediate between her mother's mathematical pedagogy and the imaginative inheritance she could not relinquish (it did, in operational form if not always in domestic-relational form).

critical_incident_database.counterfactual: An overwhelming majority of Victorian writers in comparable positions either accepted the temperamental-type frame or operated within one of the two types without articulating an integrated methodology. The deliberate articulation of imagination and mathematics as operationally distinct components of a single integrated method is structurally distinctive.
critical_incident_database.divergence_explanation: The divergence rests on the operational-articulation disposition: Ada classified her own working practice as an articulable methodology and constructed the language to articulate it, rather than treating the practice as an ineffable temperament. The same disposition produced the explicit role-separation in the August 14, 1843 letter and the explicit limitation principle in Note G.
critical_incident_database.outcome: 'Poetical science' has become one of the most-cited single phrases in the historiography of Ada's work and is the standard description of the integrated methodology her work exemplifies. The articulation also operated in her own working life as the methodological-language by which she made the dual inheritance operationally productive.

critical_incident_database.title: Publishing under the initials 'A.A.L.' — calibrated authorial signaling (September 1843)
critical_incident_database.decision: When the Sketch of the Analytical Engine with Notes was published in Taylor's Scientific Memoirs in September 1843, Ada published under the initials 'A.A.L.' (Augusta Ada Lovelace) rather than under full name, under unsigned-translator convention, or under a fully anonymous form — a calibrated middle position that simultaneously asserted authorship (the initials are unambiguous to those in her circle), preserved a degree of public anonymity (the general scientific reading public would not necessarily resolve the initials), and conformed to the period's structural pressure against women publishing scientific work under unambiguous female name.
critical_incident_database.context: The conventional options for women publishing scientific work in 1843 were (i) full anonymous publication (the typical case), (ii) publication under a male relative's name (Mary Somerville published under her own name, but this was a relatively recent change in convention), or (iii) full authorial signature (rare, though increasingly available). The Notes were structurally a hybrid — translation plus original Notes — and the authorship-signaling decision had implications for how the Notes' originality would be received.

cdm_probes.cues_noticed: Ada attended to the structural distinction between (a) the inner-circle audience (Babbage, Somerville, De Morgan, the small scientific community at Royal Society and equivalent venues) for which 'A.A.L.' was unambiguous, and (b) the broader public-periodical audience for which the initials would be unresolved. She noticed that this asymmetric resolvability would credential her in the inner circle (where it mattered for subsequent working relationships) while preserving public anonymity (where it conformed to convention and avoided the structural cost of unambiguous female authorship).
cdm_probes.active_goals: (1) Credential the Notes' originality among the audience who could evaluate it; (2) avoid the public-conventional cost of unambiguous female authorship; (3) calibrate the signaling so that subsequent attribution would resolve in her favor (it has); (4) preserve the option, in subsequent work, of publishing under fuller signature once 'A.A.L.' had been established as her credentialed identity.
cdm_probes.rejected_alternatives: She rejected full anonymous publication (would have surrendered the credentialing in the inner circle), publication under William's or Babbage's name (would have surrendered the authorship structurally), and full unambiguous publication under her name and title (would have produced public-conventional cost without proportionate credentialing benefit).
cdm_probes.situation_framing: She framed the authorship-signaling not as a binary choice but as a calibrated signal whose audience-asymmetric resolvability could be operationally optimized. The frame is the same calibrated-instrument-engineering visible in the strategic-vs-technical separation proposal of August 14, 1843.
cdm_probes.expected_outcome: She expected the inner circle to resolve 'A.A.L.' to her (it did); she expected the public to resolve it more slowly or not at all (it did, partially); she expected the calibration to be sustainable into subsequent work (the question did not arise — the Notes were the only major published scientific work she completed).

critical_incident_database.counterfactual: An overwhelming majority of women publishing technical work in 1843 chose either full anonymity or signature under a male relative's name. The deliberate calibrated middle position — initials resolvable to the inner audience, opaque to the public — is structurally distinctive.
critical_incident_database.divergence_explanation: The divergence rests on the calibrated-signaling disposition combined with the operational-self-knowledge: Ada classified the audience for the Notes as structurally heterogeneous and signaled accordingly, rather than choosing the signaling that maximized either credit or anonymity in isolation.
critical_incident_database.outcome: 'A.A.L.' became the credentialed authorial identity for the Notes within the scientific community of the period. The historiographical attribution to Ada is uncontroverted, and the calibrated initials-signaling is now itself a standard reference in the history of women's scientific authorship.

critical_incident_database.title: The 'calculus of the nervous system' proposal (1844–1845)
critical_incident_database.decision: In the months following the publication of the Notes, with the Analytical Engine not being built and no operational vehicle available for further computer-science work, Ada proposed in correspondence with Faraday and Andrew Crosse an explicit project on the 'calculus of the nervous system' — the construction of a mathematical formalism for the operation of nerves and the conscious mind, grounded in molecular-physical theories of nervous action and informed by experiments with mesmerism, electricity, and sensation.
critical_incident_database.context: The conventional options for a woman who had completed a major scientific project in 1843 were (i) cease serious work, (ii) extend the existing project (impossible because the Engine was not being built), (iii) collaborate on adjacent projects in the same domain (no operational opportunities available), or (iv) re-engage in pure-mathematical study under De Morgan's tutelage. Ada chose, instead, to construct a structurally new project in a frontier domain whose mathematization was operationally speculative.

cdm_probes.cues_noticed: Ada attended to the structural problem that the Engine project's failure to build foreclosed extension of the Notes work, and to the operational opportunity that the frontier of nervous-system science was structurally analogous to the Engine work (a domain conventionally treated as imaginative or vital, susceptible in principle to mathematization through systematic operational analysis). She noticed that her cross-domain disposition and 'poetical science' methodology were specifically suited to the kind of work the project would require.
cdm_probes.active_goals: (1) Construct an operational vehicle for continuing serious scientific work in the absence of the Engine project; (2) deploy the cross-domain synthetic style and 'poetical science' methodology in a frontier domain; (3) engage Faraday and Crosse as the structurally appropriate working partners for the experimental side of the project; (4) credential the project through the same calibrated-instrument approach that had succeeded with the Notes.
cdm_probes.rejected_alternatives: She rejected the alternative of ceasing serious work (foreclosed by her operational disposition), the alternative of pure-mathematical study (would have produced narrow specialization not aligned with her dispositional strengths), and the alternative of working only within the conventional natural-philosophy circle (would have foreclosed the frontier-domain work the 'poetical science' methodology was specifically suited to).
cdm_probes.situation_framing: She framed the post-Notes problem not as the absence of work but as the absence of an operational vehicle for the work, and she constructed a candidate vehicle in the frontier domain whose mathematization was speculative but whose dispositional fit with her methodology was strong.
cdm_probes.expected_outcome: She expected the project to produce experimental and analytical work over a multi-year horizon (it did not — the project was not pursued to completion); she expected Faraday and Crosse to be operationally useful working partners (they were, in different ways, with limitations); she expected the cross-domain methodology to be productive in the frontier domain (the historical assessment is mixed: the project was structurally premature for the available scientific instrumentation).

critical_incident_database.counterfactual: Most scientific contributors in comparable post-major-work positions (Charles Darwin between Origin and Descent, for instance) extended their existing program rather than construct a structurally new project in a frontier domain. The choice to construct a new project specifically because the existing project's vehicle had failed is structurally distinctive.
critical_incident_database.divergence_explanation: The divergence rests on the operational-vehicle disposition: Ada classified the absence of the Engine project not as the absence of the Engine but as the absence of an operational vehicle for her methodology, and she constructed a candidate vehicle accordingly. The same disposition produced, in less-celebrated form, the betting-system construction of the late 1840s.
critical_incident_database.outcome: The 'calculus of the nervous system' project produced no published artifact and was effectively abandoned by 1846 as Ada's attention shifted toward the racing project. The methodological disposition the project exemplifies — the construction of operational vehicles for the cross-domain synthetic methodology — recurs across her later work.

critical_incident_database.title: Constructing the racing betting system as quasi-mathematical project (c. 1849–1851)
critical_incident_database.decision: From approximately 1849 — having concluded that no operational vehicle was available for her 'calculus of the nervous system' project, having faced the increasing constraints of her undiagnosed but worsening illness, and having become socially involved through John Crosse with the Ascot/Epsom racing scene — Ada chose to construct, with Crosse and a small consortium, a mathematical betting system for horse racing, pursued with the same systematic operational rigor she had directed at the Notes and at the nervous-system project.
critical_incident_database.context: Horse-race betting in the period was operationally available to upper-class women through the social-occasion frame (Ascot, Epsom were socially attended by women) but was conventionally pursued at the social-amusement level rather than as a serious instrumented project. The construction of a mathematical betting system was a structurally distinct activity, generally pursued by male consortia and bookmaker-adjacent operators. Ada's involvement at the operational level was unusual; her direction of substantial intellectual and financial resources at it was distinctive.

cdm_probes.cues_noticed: Ada attended to the structural fact that horse-racing was, beneath the social-occasion frame, an environment with measurable variables (horse-form data, track conditions, stable information, bookmaker odds) susceptible in principle to mathematical modeling. She noticed the operational opportunity that John Crosse provided access to stable information and to the social network in which the betting-system could be pursued, and she noticed (less explicitly) that the project was structurally analogous to the nervous-system project: the construction of an operational vehicle for the cross-domain synthetic methodology in a domain where the operational variables were available but the mathematization was speculative.
cdm_probes.active_goals: (1) Construct an operational vehicle for continued application of the cross-domain synthetic methodology in the absence of the Engine and in the unsuccessful conclusion of the nervous-system project; (2) generate financial resources independent of her husband's estate (which she increasingly experienced as constraining); (3) deploy John Crosse as operational collaborator and personal-emotional partner; (4) sustain the operational rigor that her illness was making increasingly difficult; (5) preserve the project's autonomy from her husband and mother.
cdm_probes.rejected_alternatives: She rejected the alternative of social-amusement betting at small stakes (would not have produced an operational vehicle for the methodology), the alternative of pursuing the betting system openly with William's involvement (would have surrendered the autonomy that was load-bearing on the project's psychological function), and the alternative of returning to mathematical study under De Morgan's tutelage (which her illness was operationally foreclosing in any case).
cdm_probes.situation_framing: She framed the betting system as a quasi-mathematical project structurally analogous to her earlier work, in a domain whose operational variables were available but whose mathematization was speculative. The frame correctly identified the operational structure of the activity but incorrectly weighted its tractability — the racing data of the period was inadequate for the kind of systematic edge-construction the system required.
cdm_probes.expected_outcome: She expected the system to produce sustainable returns (it did not; the project produced large net losses, probably £2,000–£3,200, possibly more); she expected the project to provide an operational vehicle for the methodology (it provided one in form but not in productive substance); she expected the autonomy from her husband to be sustainable (it was not, ultimately — the deathbed confession in 1852 disclosed the losses and effectively ruptured the marriage).

critical_incident_database.counterfactual: An overwhelming majority of upper-class Victorian women in social-betting environments treated betting at the social-amusement level. The construction of a mathematical betting system pursued with the operational rigor Ada directed at it is distinctive and is structurally the failure-mode of the same operational-vehicle disposition that produced the Notes — applied to a domain where the structural fit was inadequate.
critical_incident_database.divergence_explanation: The divergence rests on the operational-vehicle disposition combined with the cross-domain mathematization disposition: Ada classified racing as a domain susceptible in principle to mathematical modeling and constructed an operational vehicle accordingly. The same dispositional combination produced the Notes (where the structural fit was strong) and the betting system (where it was not). The episode is therefore the failure-mode of the same operational pattern that produced the major contribution.
critical_incident_database.outcome: The betting system produced large net losses concealed from William, partial pawning of the Lovelace family diamonds (twice, with Lady Byron's intervention to redeem them), and an effective rupture of the marriage at the deathbed disclosure of 1852. The project's structural significance is as the failure-mode of the operational-vehicle disposition: the disposition that produced the Notes also produced, in a domain where the structural fit was inadequate, the most operationally damaging episode of her life.

critical_incident_database.title: Refusing the convalescent-rest regime in 1851 to complete philosophical correspondence
critical_incident_database.decision: Through 1851, with her illness now operationally severe and the medical establishment uniformly prescribing convalescent rest and reduced mental exertion, Ada chose to continue sustained philosophical and methodological correspondence (with Faraday, with Crosse, with Babbage on remaining technical questions) and to direct her remaining cognitive resources at completing what she experienced as the structural articulation of her position rather than at preserving cognitive capacity through rest.
critical_incident_database.context: By 1851 Ada's illness (almost certainly uterine cancer) was producing severe pain that opium and laudanum managed inadequately. Conventional Victorian medical advice in the position prescribed strict reduction of intellectual exertion, and her mother and husband repeatedly urged compliance. The conventional choice in the position was acceptance of the medical regime; Ada chose, under increasing constraints, to continue working.

cdm_probes.cues_noticed: Ada attended to the operational fact that the time horizon for completing intellectual work was now structurally bounded, and to the dispositional fact that her self-conception had been load-bearing on continued operational work since the 1828 Flyology project. She noticed that the convalescent-rest regime would not change the disease trajectory (the diagnosis was structurally terminal) and would foreclose the operational use of remaining time.
cdm_probes.active_goals: (1) Complete what could be completed of the philosophical-methodological articulation while operational capacity remained; (2) direct remaining cognitive resources at sustained correspondence rather than at rest; (3) calibrate the work-vs-rest tradeoff against the structurally bounded time-horizon rather than against the medical-conventional standard; (4) preserve the operational pattern of working life that had been load-bearing on her self-conception.
cdm_probes.rejected_alternatives: She rejected the convalescent-rest regime (would have foreclosed continued work without producing benefit on disease trajectory), the alternative of intermittent work at reduced intensity (would have been structurally fragmented), and the alternative of confining work to letter-writing without sustained content (would not have advanced the methodological articulation).
cdm_probes.situation_framing: She framed the structural-time-horizon as the operational variable — the question was not whether to work but how to optimize remaining cognitive capacity against a bounded horizon. The frame is consistent across her career: structural variables to be calibrated rather than conventional regimes to be accepted.
cdm_probes.expected_outcome: She expected the continued work to produce as much articulated content as the bounded time-horizon permitted (it did, partially); she expected the medical establishment and her family to continue urging the convalescent regime (they did); she expected the work-vs-rest decision to be hers to make (it was, until the final months when Lady Byron took operational control of the household).

critical_incident_database.counterfactual: An overwhelming majority of Victorian women in comparable medical situations accepted the convalescent regime under family and medical pressure. The deliberate continuation of sustained intellectual work against medical advice in the structural circumstances Ada faced is distinctive.
critical_incident_database.divergence_explanation: The divergence rests on the operational-time-horizon disposition combined with the work-as-self-conception disposition: she classified time as an operational variable to be optimized and classified continued work as load-bearing on the self-conception. The same dispositional combination informed the bed-rest mathematical study of 1829–1832.
critical_incident_database.outcome: The 1851 correspondence produced partial articulation of methodological positions but no major published work. The continued operational pattern preserved the working self-conception through the final months and is visible in the surviving deathbed-period letters.

critical_incident_database.title: The August–September 1852 confession to William of the gambling debts and Crosse involvement
critical_incident_database.decision: In an extended deathbed conversation in approximately August–September 1852, with her illness operationally terminal and the constraints of further concealment increasingly untenable, Ada chose to confess explicitly to William the full extent of the gambling debts (which had been managed through pawning of the Lovelace family diamonds and concealment from him) and the relationship with John Crosse — converting what could have been left as ambiguity at her death into explicit disclosure with operational consequences for the marriage and the household.
critical_incident_database.context: The conventional Victorian position would have been concealment to the end — preserving the marriage's surface integrity, the household's reputation, and her own posthumous public character. The structural alternative she chose required deliberate articulation of information whose disclosure she knew would rupture the marriage in its remaining weeks and would foreclose William's continuing presence at the deathbed.

cdm_probes.cues_noticed: Ada attended to the structural fact that the concealment was operationally unsustainable beyond her death (the debts would be discovered in the estate-settlement, the Crosse correspondence would be discovered in her papers) and to the operational distinction between concealment-by-default at death and explicit disclosure during life. She noticed that explicit disclosure preserved her own agency in the structural settlement (allowing her to articulate the disclosure on her own terms) where concealment-by-default would have surrendered the agency to William's posthumous discovery.
cdm_probes.active_goals: (1) Convert the structurally inevitable disclosure into one she could articulate on her own terms; (2) preserve agency in the articulation of the marriage's structural ending; (3) settle, before her death, the operational consequences for William, for John Crosse, and for the children; (4) accept the operational cost of William's withdrawal from the deathbed as the structural price of the principled disclosure.
cdm_probes.rejected_alternatives: She rejected concealment-to-death (would have surrendered agency in the structural settlement), the alternative of partial disclosure (would have left structural ambiguity unsustainable in the estate-settlement), and the alternative of confessing only to her mother (would have left William without operational information he needed for the household management).
cdm_probes.situation_framing: She framed the disclosure as a structural-honesty decision parallel to the limitation principle of Note G: explicit articulation of what was structurally true, calibrated against both concealment and over-disclosure. The frame is consistent with her career-long structural-honesty disposition.
cdm_probes.expected_outcome: She expected William to withdraw from the deathbed on disclosure (he did, leaving the room and not returning for the final weeks); she expected the marriage to be effectively closed (it was, in operational terms); she expected her own agency in the structural settlement to be preserved (it was).

critical_incident_database.counterfactual: An overwhelming majority of Victorian women in comparable circumstances chose concealment-to-death; the explicit disclosure with full operational consequences is distinctive. The closest comparator (which is itself unusual) is the deliberate posthumous-letter convention of Continental Romanticism, but Ada's disclosure was during life, not posthumous.
critical_incident_database.divergence_explanation: The divergence rests on the structural-honesty disposition (visible in the limitation principle and in the August 14, 1843 letter): Ada classified the structurally inevitable disclosure as available for explicit articulation and chose to articulate it rather than leave it to default-discovery. The same disposition produced the operational separations and explicit calibrations that distinguish her career.
critical_incident_database.outcome: William left the room on the disclosure and did not return for the final weeks. The marriage was effectively closed in operational terms. The disclosure to Lady Byron (an unspecified additional matter) further calibrated the deathbed-period family relationships. The structural settlement was Ada's, articulated on her own terms.

critical_incident_database.title: The requested burial beside Byron — symbolic settlement of the dual inheritance (1852)
critical_incident_database.decision: In the final months of her life, Ada requested explicitly that she be buried in the Byron family vault at the Church of St Mary Magdalene, Hucknall, Nottinghamshire, beside the father she had never met — a request her mother (against expectation) supported and which was carried out at her death on November 27, 1852.
critical_incident_database.context: Lady Byron had spent forty years constructing a domestic-pedagogical-public program of separation from Byron, including the curtained portrait, the prohibition of imaginative literature, the engineering of Ada's mathematical training as Byronic-antidote. Ada's request to be buried beside Byron was, in the structural terms of the family relationships, a substantial symbolic statement requiring Lady Byron's deliberate accommodation of a structural reversal.

cdm_probes.cues_noticed: Ada attended to the symbolic-structural function of burial location and to the operational opportunity that her death-time was the load-bearing moment for explicit articulation of the dual inheritance she had spent her career operationally synthesizing. She noticed that her mother's accommodation was both structurally costly and structurally available — Lady Byron, having produced the operational settlement of the deathbed period, would not refuse the symbolic settlement of the burial.
cdm_probes.active_goals: (1) Articulate the dual inheritance in symbolic-structural form at the load-bearing moment of her death; (2) settle, alongside the operational settlement of the deathbed period (the confession to William, the reconciliation with Lady Byron), the symbolic settlement of the family-historical relationships; (3) construct the posthumous-historical record on her own terms, since once the burial was carried out it would be structurally permanent.
cdm_probes.rejected_alternatives: She rejected the alternative of conventional burial in a Lovelace family vault (would have submerged the dual inheritance), the alternative of burial beside Lady Byron (would have surrendered the Byronic component), and the alternative of leaving the question open (would have surrendered the symbolic-structural articulation to her mother's posthumous decision).
cdm_probes.situation_framing: She framed her own death not only as a personal-medical event but as the load-bearing structural moment for the articulation of the dual-inheritance synthesis her career had operationally enacted. The frame is consistent with the operational-articulation disposition visible in 'poetical science' and in the August 14, 1843 letter to Babbage.
cdm_probes.expected_outcome: She expected Lady Byron to accommodate the request (Lady Byron did); she expected the burial to be carried out (it was); she expected the symbolic articulation to be load-bearing on subsequent reception of her life and work (it has been, decisively).

critical_incident_database.counterfactual: An overwhelming majority of Victorian women in comparable circumstances would have left burial-location decisions to family-conventional default. The deliberate request, against the family-pedagogical structure of forty years, is distinctive and is the most articulated single posthumous-symbolic decision in her surviving record.
critical_incident_database.divergence_explanation: The divergence rests on the operational-articulation disposition: Ada classified her death as the load-bearing moment for the symbolic articulation of the dual inheritance and constructed the symbolic instrument (the burial location) accordingly. The same disposition produced the explicit role-separation of August 1843 and the explicit limitation principle of Note G.
critical_incident_database.outcome: Ada was buried in the Byron family vault at Hucknall on December 3, 1852, beside the father she had never met. The symbolic settlement has been load-bearing on the subsequent biographical and historiographical reception of her life and work, and is the structural counterpart to the operational settlement her career enacted.

critical_incident_database.title: Refusing in 1841 the suggestion of a light scientific accomplishment-style work
critical_incident_database.decision: In approximately 1841, in the context of her resumed mathematical work under De Morgan and her active correspondence with Somerville and Babbage, Ada was repeatedly counseled — by family advisers and within the conventional structure of advice to a Victorian woman of her position — toward the production of light scientific or accomplishment-style work (a popular natural-philosophy treatise, a translation suitable for educated women readers, a literary-scientific essay) rather than toward the sustained technical work the De Morgan tutorial implied. She rejected the counsel and continued the technical preparation, choosing to direct her preparation specifically at sustained engagement with the Engine work that the post-1840 Babbage correspondence was making operationally available.
critical_incident_database.context: The structural pressure toward accomplishment-style work was pervasive in Ada's social environment: it was the conventional channel for women of her class who wished to participate in scientific culture, and it carried no structural risk to marriage, motherhood, or class-position. The technical-preparation alternative carried structural risks (the strain on health that De Morgan would later document, the social-conventional cost of unambiguous scientific work, the operational cost of compressed schedule and country-estate concentration) but offered the load-bearing capacity for the kind of work the Engine encounter was preparing.

cdm_probes.cues_noticed: Ada attended to the structural distinction between accomplishment-style work (which would have credentialed her in a conventionally available channel but would not have enabled the kind of work she actually wished to do) and technical preparation (which carried structural costs but enabled the load-bearing capacity). She noticed that the operational target — engagement with the Engine — required the technical preparation and would not be served by the accomplishment-style alternative.
cdm_probes.active_goals: (1) Direct preparation specifically at the operational target the Engine work represented; (2) refuse the accomplishment-style channel as structurally inadequate to the target; (3) absorb the structural costs (health-strain, social-conventional friction, household compression) as the operational price of the principled choice; (4) preserve the ability to direct her own preparation against external counsel.
cdm_probes.rejected_alternatives: She rejected the accomplishment-style channel (would have foreclosed the load-bearing capacity), the alternative of dual preparation (would have diluted the technical work), and the alternative of postponing the technical preparation until later in life (would have lost the operational alignment with the Babbage correspondence).
cdm_probes.situation_framing: She framed the choice not as a question of personal preference but as a structural-target alignment: the operational target required the technical preparation, and the accomplishment-style channel was structurally inadequate to the target. The frame is consistent with the operational-target alignment visible in the model-choice of 1833.
cdm_probes.expected_outcome: She expected the technical preparation to be load-bearing on subsequent work (it was, decisively, in the 1843 Notes); she expected the structural costs to be absorbable (they were, with substantial health-strain); she expected the family advisers to continue urging the accomplishment-style alternative (they did).

critical_incident_database.counterfactual: An overwhelming majority of women of comparable position in 1841 who participated in scientific culture did so through the accomplishment-style channel. The deliberate refusal of the channel and direct preparation for sustained technical work is structurally distinctive.
critical_incident_database.divergence_explanation: The divergence rests on the operational-target alignment disposition: Ada classified the choice by the alignment with the operational target rather than by the conventional structure of available channels. The same disposition produced the choice of Somerville-style synthesis over Cambridge specialization in 1833.
critical_incident_database.outcome: The continued technical preparation under De Morgan from 1840 through 1842 produced the foundations on which the 1843 Notes rested. The accomplishment-style alternative would have foreclosed the major contribution.

critical_incident_database.title: The early 1843 correction of the Bernoulli table error
critical_incident_database.decision: In summer 1843, during the construction of the Bernoulli-numbers programming example in Note G, Babbage discovered an error in Ada's draft table; the correspondence (Lovelace–Babbage letters of July 1843) records Ada's reaction. Rather than treating the error as occasion for cessation or for Babbage's takeover of the table-construction, she rebuilt the entire table from foundations, wrote to Babbage in operational frustration ('I have been working incessantly... I have been like a fiend at it'), and retained authorial control of the program-construction while accepting Babbage's correction of the underlying calculation step.
critical_incident_database.context: The conventional response in the position would have been either (i) acceptance of Babbage's takeover of the corrected table (would have surrendered authorial control of the most load-bearing single artifact in the publication) or (ii) cessation of the example with substitution of a simpler problem (would have weakened the demonstration of the Engine's programming capacity). Ada chose neither.

cdm_probes.cues_noticed: Ada attended to the operational distinction between the underlying mathematical method (Babbage's domain, where his correction was load-bearing) and the program-construction and table-form (her domain, where authorial control was load-bearing on the credentialing of the Notes' originality). She noticed that the rebuild was operationally tractable within the press deadline if she committed to compressed-intensive working.
cdm_probes.active_goals: (1) Correct the error in the underlying calculation (accepting Babbage's correction); (2) preserve authorial control of the program-construction and table-form by rebuilding rather than ceding; (3) credential the Bernoulli example as the load-bearing artifact of the Notes through her own completed reconstruction; (4) maintain the working partnership with Babbage through the correction without ceding the authorial structure.
cdm_probes.rejected_alternatives: She rejected Babbage's takeover of the corrected table, substitution of a simpler programming example, and the alternative of confining her authorial role to the surrounding exposition while ceding the table-construction. She rejected each on the operational ground that the Bernoulli table was the load-bearing artifact and that authorial control was structurally load-bearing on the Notes' credentialing.
cdm_probes.situation_framing: She framed the error not as occasion for ceding authorial control but as occasion for compressed-intensive reconstruction. The frame is consistent with the operational-target disposition: the operational target was the credentialing artifact, and the structural cost of reconstruction was absorbable.
cdm_probes.expected_outcome: She expected to complete the rebuilt table within the press deadline (she did, with substantial compressed effort); she expected authorial control of the table to be preserved (it was, and the historiographical record assigns the program-construction and table-form to her); she expected the working partnership to absorb the correction-and-rebuild cycle (it did, though the strain contributed to the August 14 conflict).

critical_incident_database.counterfactual: An overwhelming majority of collaborators in the position would have accepted the principal-collaborator's takeover of the corrected artifact, particularly under press-deadline pressure. The choice to rebuild while preserving authorial control is distinctive.
critical_incident_database.divergence_explanation: The divergence rests on the credentialing-instrument disposition combined with the operational-self-knowledge: Ada classified the table as the load-bearing credentialing artifact and her authorial control as the structurally load-bearing variable, and she absorbed the reconstruction cost rather than cede the structural variable.
critical_incident_database.outcome: The corrected and rebuilt Bernoulli table was completed on schedule and is the artifact most often cited as the first computer program. Authorial control of the program-construction is uncontroverted in the historiographical record.

critical_incident_database.title: Constructing the Lady Byron deathbed reconciliation on Lady Byron's terms (1852)
critical_incident_database.decision: In the final months of 1852 — having confessed to William the gambling debts and the Crosse involvement (incident-016), and having requested burial beside Byron (incident-017) — Ada chose to construct the deathbed reconciliation with Lady Byron substantially on Lady Byron's terms: she made an unspecified additional confession (variously interpreted as further information about the Crosse affair or about her father's circle) that confirmed Lady Byron's structural account of the family, accepted Lady Byron's operational control of the household and medical care in the final weeks, and permitted the exclusion of William from the deathbed in the final period.
critical_incident_database.context: Forty years of Lady Byron's pedagogical and domestic program had constructed an account of the family — the Byron-as-danger account — that Ada's life had operationally complicated and at times resisted. The deathbed reconciliation could have been constructed on Ada's terms (insisting on her own account of the dual inheritance, refusing to confirm her mother's structural account, preserving William's place at the deathbed) or on Lady Byron's terms; Ada chose the latter even while preserving (in the burial-request) the symbolic articulation of the dual inheritance.

cdm_probes.cues_noticed: Ada attended to the operational distinction between the symbolic settlement (the burial request, where she preserved the dual-inheritance articulation) and the relational settlement (with Lady Byron, where confirmation of her mother's account was operationally load-bearing on the surviving relationships of the household, including with the children Lady Byron would substantially raise). She noticed that the two settlements could be constructed on different terms without inconsistency, since they operated in distinct structural domains.
cdm_probes.active_goals: (1) Construct the relational settlement with Lady Byron in terms that preserved the surviving relationships of the household; (2) preserve, separately, the symbolic settlement of the dual inheritance through the burial request; (3) accept the operational cost of confirming Lady Byron's structural account in exchange for the operational benefit of the surviving relationships; (4) calibrate the two settlements as structurally distinct rather than as competing.
cdm_probes.rejected_alternatives: She rejected insisting on her own account of the family in the deathbed period (would have foreclosed the surviving relationships and would have left her children's upbringing without Lady Byron's structural support), the alternative of refusing the reconciliation entirely (operationally untenable), and the alternative of constructing both settlements on the same terms (would have collapsed the distinction between symbolic and relational domains).
cdm_probes.situation_framing: She framed the deathbed period as containing structurally distinct settlements that could be constructed on different terms without inconsistency. The frame is consistent with the calibrated-instrument-engineering visible across her career (the August 14, 1843 letter, the 'A.A.L.' authorship signaling).
cdm_probes.expected_outcome: She expected the relational settlement to preserve the surviving relationships of the household (it did — Lady Byron substantially raised the three children); she expected the symbolic settlement to articulate the dual inheritance (it did, in the burial); she expected the two settlements to coexist without operational contradiction (they have, in the historiographical reception).

critical_incident_database.counterfactual: Either insisting on her own account of the family or refusing reconciliation entirely would have been the more conventional dispositions in the position. The deliberate construction of structurally distinct settlements on different terms — relational on the mother's, symbolic on her own — is distinctive.
critical_incident_database.divergence_explanation: The divergence rests on the calibrated-instrument-engineering disposition combined with the operational-self-knowledge: Ada classified the deathbed period as containing structurally distinct settlement-domains and configured each on its own load-bearing variables.
critical_incident_database.outcome: Lady Byron raised the three Lovelace children substantially after Ada's death, sustaining the household relationships the relational settlement preserved. The symbolic settlement (the Hucknall burial) articulated the dual inheritance. Both settlements operated as Ada had configured them.

critical_incident_database.title: The Wheatstone-instigated translation as engineered occasion (early 1843)
critical_incident_database.decision: In early 1843, Charles Wheatstone — having read Menabrea's October 1842 French-language exposition of the Analytical Engine in the Bibliothèque universelle de Genève — suggested that Ada translate the article into English for publication in Taylor's Scientific Memoirs. Ada accepted the suggestion, completed the translation, and presented it to Babbage. The structural decision was the acceptance of the translator-role frame as the operational opportunity through which her own original work could be introduced — the engineered occasion that the subsequent Notes-construction required.
critical_incident_database.context: Direct publication of an original paper on the Analytical Engine in 1843 by a 27-year-old woman would have been structurally difficult: it would have required navigating both the gender-conventional barriers to scientific authorship and the absence of an existing public discourse on the Engine into which an original paper could be slotted. The translator-role framing converted the structural problem: it provided an external warrant (a recognized French-language source) into which the Notes could be appended, leveraged the legitimate scientific occasion of translation, and routed her authorial introduction through a structurally less-contested channel.

cdm_probes.cues_noticed: Ada attended to the operational distinction between direct authorial publication (structurally contested) and translator-role publication (structurally less contested), and to Wheatstone's suggestion as the available operational occasion. She noticed that the translation, once completed, would create an operational opportunity to append original Notes that would not have been available through direct publication.
cdm_probes.active_goals: (1) Accept Wheatstone's suggestion as the structurally available occasion; (2) complete the translation at a quality that would credential the appended Notes; (3) preserve the operational option of expanding the Notes once the translation was complete; (4) calibrate the role-framing so that subsequent expansion would be structurally available rather than externally foreclosed.
cdm_probes.rejected_alternatives: She rejected the alternative of refusing the translation suggestion (would have lost the operational occasion), the alternative of writing an original paper for separate publication (structurally contested in 1843 in ways the translation was not), and the alternative of completing the translation without expansion (would have foreclosed the appended-Notes opportunity).
cdm_probes.situation_framing: She framed Wheatstone's suggestion as an engineered occasion: the translator-role provided structural cover under which expanded original work could be conducted, and the cover was operationally exploitable specifically because the translation was a legitimate occasion in its own right.
cdm_probes.expected_outcome: She expected the translation to provide the operational occasion for the Notes (it did, decisively); she expected the expansion to be tolerated by Babbage and the publisher (it was); she expected the translator-role framing to credential the publication while preserving the original-work content (it did).

critical_incident_database.counterfactual: Most contributors in the position would have completed the translation as a conventional translator-role assignment without expansion. The recognition of the translation as engineered-occasion-for-original-work, and the subsequent expansion to the structurally distinctive Notes, is distinctive.
critical_incident_database.divergence_explanation: The divergence rests on the opportunity-recognition disposition (visible from the 1833 Difference Engine demonstration): Ada classified Wheatstone's suggestion not as a translator-role assignment but as an operational occasion that contained the structural opportunity for original work, and she configured her response to exploit the opportunity.
critical_incident_database.outcome: The translation was completed and the expansion to the Notes followed. The Notes are the singular major artifact of her career and the load-bearing publication.

critical_incident_database.title: Engaging Andrew Crosse's experimental laboratory at Fyne Court (1844)
critical_incident_database.decision: In 1844, in the context of the proposed 'calculus of the nervous system' project (incident-013), Ada visited Andrew Crosse's experimental laboratory at Fyne Court in Somerset and engaged with his ongoing work on electrocrystallization, atmospheric electricity, and the so-called 'Acari Crossii' (the apparent emergence of mites in mineral-electric experiments, a phenomenon contemporary controversy attributed variously to spontaneous generation or contamination). The decision to engage seriously with Crosse's frontier-experimental work — conducted by an isolated provincial gentleman-scientist, controversial in the metropolitan scientific establishment, and operating well outside the credentialed scientific community Ada otherwise inhabited — represents her structural willingness to engage with frontier-experimental work on its operational merits rather than on its credentialing position.
critical_incident_database.context: The metropolitan scientific establishment of 1844 (the Royal Society circle, the Babbage-Herschel network, Faraday at the Royal Institution) treated Crosse's work with substantial skepticism, particularly the Acari claims. The conventional disposition for someone of Ada's credentialing-anchored position would have been to keep distance from Crosse's work to avoid contamination of her scientific credentialing. The choice to engage Crosse's laboratory as a serious working venue is operationally distinctive.

cdm_probes.cues_noticed: Ada attended to the operational distinction between credentialing-anchored science (which would foreclose engagement with Crosse) and frontier-experimental science (where Crosse's work was operationally available regardless of credentialing position). She noticed that the cross-domain methodology she was attempting to deploy in the nervous-system project required engagement with the kinds of frontier work credentialed science was structurally unable to host. She noticed that Crosse's laboratory and family circle (including John Crosse, with whom she would later become involved) provided an operational venue not available in the metropolitan establishment.
cdm_probes.active_goals: (1) Engage frontier-experimental work as operationally relevant to the nervous-system project; (2) accept the credentialing risk of association with Crosse as the structural cost of the engagement; (3) construct the Crosse household as an operational working venue distinct from the metropolitan circles; (4) preserve the methodological autonomy that the cross-domain work required.
cdm_probes.rejected_alternatives: She rejected the conventional credentialing-protective disposition (would have foreclosed the frontier engagement), the alternative of confining engagement with Crosse to social correspondence (would not have produced operational working content), and the alternative of pursuing the nervous-system work entirely within the metropolitan circles (would have foreclosed the frontier-experimental component).
cdm_probes.situation_framing: She framed Crosse's laboratory not by its credentialing position but by its operational fit with the cross-domain frontier work she was attempting. The frame is consistent with the operational-target alignment disposition visible across her career.
cdm_probes.expected_outcome: She expected the Crosse engagement to provide operational content for the nervous-system project (it did, partially — the project itself was structurally premature); she expected the credentialing cost to be absorbable (it was, in her then-position); she expected the John Crosse working-and-personal involvement to be productive (it was, partially, and produced the betting-system project years later, with mixed operational consequences).

critical_incident_database.counterfactual: Most credentialing-anchored scientific contributors of the period kept structural distance from Crosse's work. The deliberate engagement on operational-merit grounds is distinctive and is consistent with the cross-domain methodological disposition.
critical_incident_database.divergence_explanation: The divergence rests on the operational-target alignment disposition combined with the cross-domain disposition: Ada classified the working venue by its operational fit with the methodological target rather than by its credentialing position. The same disposition produced engagement with Mesmeric and 'molecular' theories of nervous action.
critical_incident_database.outcome: The Crosse engagement produced the working partnership with John Crosse that became the operational and personal venue of the late 1840s, including the betting-system project and the personal involvement disclosed at the deathbed in 1852.

critical_incident_database.title: Refusing in early 1840s society to perform the conventional 'Byron's daughter' role
critical_incident_database.decision: Across the early 1840s, in the social settings (London salons, country-house gatherings, the marginal scientific-society-and-aristocratic intersections of the period) where she was repeatedly invited to perform the conventional 'Byron's daughter' role — to allude to her father's poetry, to display the temperamental-Byronic associations the public attached to her, to credential her presence at scientific occasions through the literary-celebrity inheritance — Ada chose to refuse the role and to credential her presence on her own scientific terms, repeatedly redirecting conversations to the technical content of her work, declining to discuss Byron except in the most controlled circumstances, and signaling through dress and conduct an austere mathematical-scientific identity rather than the literary-celebrity one.
critical_incident_database.context: The structural pressure to perform the Byron's-daughter role was pervasive: it was the conventional channel through which her public presence was operationally credentialed in the period, and refusing the role required substantial social-conventional friction. The alternative — credentialing her presence on her own scientific terms — required the construction of an operational identity not available by inheritance.

cdm_probes.cues_noticed: Ada attended to the structural distinction between inherited credentialing (Byron's daughter, available without operational construction but structurally inadequate to the scientific work she actually wanted to do) and constructed credentialing (built through the technical work, the De Morgan tutorial, the Notes, the Babbage partnership). She noticed that performing the inherited role would foreclose the constructed one in scientific settings, since the two roles operated on incompatible signaling.
cdm_probes.active_goals: (1) Construct her public-scientific identity on her own operational terms; (2) refuse the inherited literary-celebrity role to the extent operationally possible without breaching social-conventional constraints; (3) signal through dress, conduct, and conversation the austere mathematical-scientific identity rather than the literary-celebrity one; (4) preserve the option of strategic invocation of the inheritance in specifically calibrated circumstances.
cdm_probes.rejected_alternatives: She rejected performing the conventional role (would have foreclosed the constructed identity), the alternative of dual-role performance (would have diluted the constructed identity), and the alternative of complete withdrawal from the social settings (would have foreclosed the operational venues where the constructed identity could be exercised).
cdm_probes.situation_framing: She framed the choice as a credentialing-construction problem: the constructed identity required operationally consistent signaling across social settings, and the inherited role was structurally incompatible with the construction. The frame is consistent with the operational-self-knowledge and calibrated-signaling dispositions.
cdm_probes.expected_outcome: She expected the constructed identity to credential her in the scientific settings (it did, partially); she expected the social-conventional friction of refusing the inherited role to be absorbable (it was); she expected to preserve the option of strategic invocation of the inheritance (she did, occasionally, in specific calibrated circumstances).

critical_incident_database.counterfactual: Most public daughters of literary celebrities in the period performed the inherited role. The deliberate refusal of the role in favor of an operationally constructed scientific identity is distinctive and is consistent with the operational-self-knowledge disposition.
critical_incident_database.divergence_explanation: The divergence rests on the operational-self-knowledge disposition combined with the calibrated-signaling disposition (visible in the 'A.A.L.' authorship signaling): Ada classified her public identity as a constructable operational variable rather than as an inheritable position, and configured the signaling accordingly.
critical_incident_database.outcome: The constructed scientific identity credentialed her presence in the Babbage-Somerville-De Morgan circles and supported the operational working relationships that produced the Notes. The Byron-inheritance was preserved as a strategic resource for specifically calibrated circumstances and was symbolically reclaimed in the deathbed burial decision.

critical_incident_database.title: The decision (June 1843) to retain Note G's limitation principle against Babbage's preference for stronger capability claims
critical_incident_database.decision: In the late stages of Notes composition in June 1843, with Babbage urging stronger capability claims to credential the Engine in the eyes of potential government patrons and skeptical reviewers, Ada chose to retain — and indeed to strengthen — the explicit limitation principle of Note G ('The Analytical Engine has no pretensions whatever to originate any thing'), refusing the operational pressure to dilute or remove it.
critical_incident_database.context: Babbage's strategic-political situation in mid-1843 made stronger capability claims operationally attractive: government patronage had been withdrawn, the engineering project required substantial new funding, and credentialing the Engine in the eyes of potential patrons required strong public claims. The conventional collaborator's response would have been accommodation; Ada's was the opposite.

cdm_probes.cues_noticed: Ada attended to the structural distinction between short-term credentialing (which would have been served by stronger capability claims) and long-term structural credibility (which required the principled limitation alongside the principled capability). She noticed that diluting the limitation would have weakened the capability claim by attaching to it an embarrassed limitation rather than a principled one (incident-009).
cdm_probes.active_goals: (1) Retain the principled limitation as load-bearing on the long-term credibility of the capability claims; (2) refuse the operational pressure for short-term credentialing-strengthening that would damage long-term structural credibility; (3) preserve the calibrated structural-honesty position; (4) accept the cost in working-relationship friction with Babbage.
cdm_probes.rejected_alternatives: She rejected dilution of the limitation, removal of the limitation, and the alternative of confining the limitation to a less prominent position in the Notes. Each would have served Babbage's short-term credentialing interest at the structural cost of the calibrated position.
cdm_probes.situation_framing: She framed the choice as a long-term-credibility decision rather than as a short-term-credentialing accommodation. The frame is consistent with the structural-honesty disposition visible across her career.
cdm_probes.expected_outcome: She expected the limitation principle to be load-bearing on long-term credibility (it has been, decisively, across the entire subsequent history of computing); she expected the short-term credentialing cost to be absorbable (it was, partially); she expected Babbage to accommodate the principled position (he did, with friction).

critical_incident_database.counterfactual: Most collaborators in the position would have accommodated the principal-collaborator's preference under operational pressure, particularly given Babbage's strategic-political situation. The principled retention of the limitation against the operational pressure is distinctive.
critical_incident_database.divergence_explanation: The divergence rests on the structural-honesty disposition (visible in the limitation-principle articulation, in the August 14, 1843 letter, in the deathbed confession of 1852): Ada classified long-term structural credibility as the load-bearing variable and refused short-term operational pressure that would have damaged it.
critical_incident_database.outcome: Note G's limitation principle was retained in the published form, became Lady Lovelace's Objection in Turing's 1950 paper, and has been load-bearing on the philosophy of artificial intelligence ever since.

critical_incident_database.title: Refusing to attend Babbage's funeral plans for further joint Engine work in 1844
critical_incident_database.decision: In late 1843 and through 1844, in the aftermath of the August 14 conflict and the publication of the Notes, Babbage repeatedly attempted to engage Ada in further joint Engine-related work — in revision and extension of the Notes, in the preparation of additional technical material, and in the strategic-political project of resurrecting government funding. Ada chose to disengage substantially from the joint-engineering project while preserving the personal-correspondence relationship: she declined to commit operational time to further Engine-technical work, redirected her own preparation toward the nervous-system project (incident-013), and effectively closed the partnership-as-vehicle without formally rupturing the personal relationship.
critical_incident_database.context: The conventional disposition in the position would have been continuation of the partnership at the operational level the August 14 conflict had configured, on the operational ground that the Engine work was the singular venue of Ada's credentialed scientific contribution. The choice to disengage operationally while preserving the personal relationship is structurally distinctive.

cdm_probes.cues_noticed: Ada attended to the structural fact that the August 14 letter's role-separation proposal had been rejected, and that the partnership therefore did not contain the operational structure on which sustained joint work could be productively organized. She noticed that Babbage's strategic-political failures continued unrelieved, and that further joint work would absorb her operational capacity without producing proportionate output. She noticed that the nervous-system project (although operationally speculative) provided an alternative methodological vehicle.
cdm_probes.active_goals: (1) Disengage operationally from the joint-engineering project on the structural ground that the partnership lacked the configuration for sustained productive work; (2) preserve the personal correspondence as a working relationship distinct from the partnership; (3) redirect operational capacity toward an alternative methodological vehicle; (4) avoid the conventional cost of formal partnership-rupture.
cdm_probes.rejected_alternatives: She rejected continuation of the partnership at the August 14 configuration (operationally unproductive), formal rupture of the relationship (unnecessary cost), and the alternative of attempting renegotiation of the role-separation proposal (Babbage had already rejected it).
cdm_probes.situation_framing: She framed the partnership and the personal relationship as structurally distinct domains that could be configured on different terms — the partnership disengaged, the personal relationship preserved. The frame is consistent with the calibrated-instrument disposition.
cdm_probes.expected_outcome: She expected the operational disengagement to free capacity for the alternative project (it did); she expected the personal relationship to survive the disengagement (it did, in attenuated form); she expected Babbage to continue urging joint work (he did, intermittently, across the next several years).

critical_incident_database.counterfactual: Most collaborators in the position would have either continued the operationally unproductive partnership under social-conventional pressure or formally ruptured the relationship. The deliberate operational disengagement combined with personal-relationship preservation is structurally distinctive.
critical_incident_database.divergence_explanation: The divergence rests on the calibrated-instrument disposition combined with the operational-vehicle disposition: Ada classified the partnership and the personal relationship as structurally distinct domains and configured each on its own load-bearing variables.
critical_incident_database.outcome: The partnership-as-vehicle was effectively closed; the personal correspondence continued in attenuated form. No further joint Engine-related work was undertaken; the Notes remain the singular product of the partnership.

critical_incident_database.title: Insisting on autograph notes during the Notes composition rather than dictation (1843)
critical_incident_database.decision: Across the intense composition period of the Notes between February and August 1843, with the household's domestic constraints producing pressure toward dictation-to-amanuensis as a structurally faster mode (a mode used by other women writers of the period, and offered as available by William and her staff), Ada chose to write the Notes primarily in her own hand, sustaining sustained autograph composition through the compressed schedule (incident-005) rather than ceding the writing-process to dictation.
critical_incident_database.context: Dictation-to-amanuensis was a structurally available mode in the period and was used by other women writers (including, in some accounts, Mary Somerville on parts of On the Connexion). The dictation mode would have permitted compressed-time productivity but at the structural cost of separating the writing-process from the thinking-process, since dictation tends to produce articulated rather than tentative text.

cdm_probes.cues_noticed: Ada attended to the operational distinction between articulated dictation (which produces text but reduces the document's role in the thinking-process) and autograph composition (slower but operationally inseparable from the working-out of the conceptual content). She noticed that the Notes' content (Note A's reframe, Note G's table-construction, the limitation principle) was operationally exploratory in ways dictation would have foreclosed.
cdm_probes.active_goals: (1) Preserve the writing-process as the load-bearing thinking-instrument for the conceptual content; (2) sustain autograph composition through the compressed schedule despite the productivity cost; (3) reserve dictation only for material that was articulated already (correspondence, formal exposition where the content was settled); (4) preserve the operational pattern by which the document and the thinking were inseparable.
cdm_probes.rejected_alternatives: She rejected dictation as the primary composition mode (would have foreclosed the thinking-instrument function), the alternative of mixed-mode composition (would have fragmented the thinking-process), and the alternative of slower-paced autograph composition that would have missed the press deadline (operationally infeasible).
cdm_probes.situation_framing: She framed writing as the load-bearing thinking-instrument rather than as a productivity mode, with autograph composition the operationally specific form. The frame is consistent with the documentation-as-thinking pattern visible from the 1828 Flyology manuscript.
cdm_probes.expected_outcome: She expected autograph composition to sustain the thinking-process through the conceptual content of the Notes (it did); she expected the productivity cost to be absorbable through compressed-intensive working (it was, with substantial health-strain); she expected the manuscript-form of the Notes to be operationally productive in subsequent revision (it was).

critical_incident_database.counterfactual: Many women writers of the period used dictation under comparable household constraints. The principled retention of autograph composition as the thinking-instrument, against the productivity-cost, is distinctive.
critical_incident_database.divergence_explanation: The divergence rests on the documentation-as-thinking disposition (visible from the 1828 Flyology manuscript): Ada classified writing as the operational thinking-instrument rather than as a productivity mode and refused to surrender the operational structure under productivity-pressure.
critical_incident_database.outcome: The Notes were composed primarily in autograph form across February to August 1843. The manuscript survives in the British Library and is the principal documentary record of the composition-process.

critical_incident_database.title: Resisting Faraday's redirection toward conventional natural-philosophy correspondence (1844)
critical_incident_database.decision: In the post-Notes correspondence with Michael Faraday from late 1843 through 1844, with Faraday — who had read and admired the Notes — initially attempting to redirect the correspondence toward conventional natural-philosophy topics (electromagnetic phenomena in the Faraday-canonical frame, the standard topics of Royal Institution Friday Discourses), Ada chose to redirect the correspondence back toward the cross-domain frontier topics she actually wanted to pursue (the calculus of the nervous system, the mathematization of mesmerism, the molecular theory of nervous action), preserving the cross-domain methodological direction even at the cost of partial misalignment with Faraday's canonical interests.
critical_incident_database.context: Faraday was, in 1844, the most credentialed natural-philosopher available to Ada and the obvious anchor for a credentialed continuation of her scientific work. Conventional disposition would have been accommodation of Faraday's preferred topics in exchange for the credentialing benefit. The choice to redirect the correspondence toward the frontier topics, accepting partial misalignment, is structurally consistent with the cross-domain disposition.

cdm_probes.cues_noticed: Ada attended to the structural distinction between credentialing-anchored correspondence (which would have produced credentialing benefit but would not have served the methodological project) and cross-domain frontier correspondence (which would have served the methodological project but at the credentialing cost). She noticed that Faraday's intellectual range was sufficient to sustain at least partial engagement with the frontier topics, and that the methodological project required this engagement.
cdm_probes.active_goals: (1) Preserve the cross-domain methodological direction in the post-Notes correspondence; (2) engage Faraday at the level of his actual interest in the frontier topics; (3) accept the partial misalignment with his canonical interests as the structural cost of the methodological direction; (4) sustain the credentialing benefit at the level the partial alignment would support.
cdm_probes.rejected_alternatives: She rejected accommodation of Faraday's canonical interests as the primary direction of the correspondence (would have surrendered the methodological direction), the alternative of foreclosing the correspondence entirely (would have lost the available credentialing partial-anchor), and the alternative of attempting to convert Faraday into a frontier-correspondent in his own right (operationally infeasible).
cdm_probes.situation_framing: She framed the correspondence as a methodologically directed engagement, with the methodological direction the load-bearing variable and the credentialing benefit the secondary variable. The frame is consistent with the operational-target alignment disposition.
cdm_probes.expected_outcome: She expected partial alignment with Faraday at the level his interests permitted (it was achieved); she expected the methodological direction to be preserved (it was); she expected the credentialing benefit to be partial rather than full (it was).

critical_incident_database.counterfactual: Most contributors with access to Faraday in the position would have accommodated his canonical interests for the credentialing benefit. The deliberate redirection toward the frontier topics, accepting partial misalignment, is distinctive.
critical_incident_database.divergence_explanation: The divergence rests on the operational-target alignment disposition combined with the cross-domain disposition: Ada classified the correspondence by the methodological direction rather than by the credentialing anchor, and configured engagement accordingly.
critical_incident_database.outcome: The Faraday correspondence produced partial alignment on the frontier topics and sustained at the credentialing-partial level Ada had configured. No major joint work resulted, but the correspondence sustained the methodological direction across the post-Notes years.

critical_incident_database.title: Choosing in 1843 not to seek Lady Byron's intervention to credential the Notes
critical_incident_database.decision: Across the composition and publication of the Notes between February and September 1843, Ada chose not to seek Lady Byron's social-political intervention to credential the publication — declining to ask her mother to mobilize the Byron-Milbanke aristocratic network on behalf of the Notes' reception, declining to request introductions to Royal Society or governmental figures through her mother's channels, and conducting the publication's strategic positioning entirely through her own and Babbage's networks (and conspicuously not through her mother's).
critical_incident_database.context: Lady Byron's network was operationally substantial and could have been mobilized to credential the Notes' reception in establishment circles. Conventional disposition for a daughter in the position would have been to seek the maternal social-political support, both as conventionally appropriate and as operationally productive. The choice to conduct the publication's strategic positioning independently of her mother's network is structurally consistent with the operational-self-knowledge disposition.

cdm_probes.cues_noticed: Ada attended to the structural distinction between credentialing through her mother's network (which would have anchored the Notes to the inherited family-political position) and credentialing through her own and Babbage's networks (which would have anchored the Notes to the constructed scientific identity). She noticed that her mother's intervention would have implicitly framed the Notes as a maternal-supervised production rather than as an independent contribution, and that the framing would have been operationally costly to the constructed identity.
cdm_probes.active_goals: (1) Credential the Notes through the constructed scientific identity rather than through the inherited family-political position; (2) preserve the operational independence of the publication from the maternal pedagogical-supervisory frame; (3) conduct the strategic positioning through her own and Babbage's networks; (4) accept the credentialing cost of forgoing her mother's network as the structural price of the constructed-identity anchor.
cdm_probes.rejected_alternatives: She rejected mobilization of Lady Byron's network for credentialing (would have anchored the Notes to the inherited position), the alternative of partial use of the maternal network for some functions (would have been structurally inconsistent with the constructed-identity anchor), and the alternative of conventional gratitude-acknowledgment of her mother's role in the publication (would have implied a structural role that did not, in fact, exist).
cdm_probes.situation_framing: She framed the credentialing as a structural-anchor decision in which the constructed identity required independence from the inherited family-political position. The frame is consistent with the operational-self-knowledge disposition and with the refusal of the conventional Byron's-daughter role.
cdm_probes.expected_outcome: She expected the constructed-identity anchor to be operationally adequate to credential the Notes (it was, partially); she expected the credentialing cost of forgoing the maternal network to be absorbable (it was); she expected Lady Byron to recognize the structural choice without operational rupture (Lady Byron did, with mixed feelings).

critical_incident_database.counterfactual: Most daughters in comparable position would have mobilized the maternal network for credentialing on grounds of conventional appropriateness and operational productivity. The deliberate independence from the maternal network is distinctive and is consistent with the operational-self-knowledge disposition.
critical_incident_database.divergence_explanation: The divergence rests on the operational-self-knowledge disposition combined with the constructed-identity disposition (visible in the refusal of the conventional Byron's-daughter role): Ada classified the credentialing-anchor as a structural-identity decision and chose the anchor that aligned with the constructed identity rather than the inherited position.
critical_incident_database.outcome: The Notes were credentialed through Ada's and Babbage's networks. Lady Byron recognized the structural choice and did not attempt to redirect the credentialing through her own channels. The constructed-identity anchor was preserved across the publication.
